things.name: Carlingford Medieval Village
things.description: Perfectly preserved Anglo-Norman village on Carlingford Lough — King John's Castle, Taaffe's Castle and the finest medieval streetscape in Leinster

things.name: Proleek Dolmen
things.description: Remarkable Neolithic portal tomb with a 40-tonne capstone — tradition says making a pebble land on top grants a wish

things.name: County Louth Museum (Dundalk Museum)
things.description: The main museum for Ireland's smallest county — permanent exhibitions on the Cooley Peninsula, the Táin and local heritage

things.name: Cooley Peninsula (Scenic Drive)
things.description: Dramatic peninsula between Carlingford Lough and Dundalk Bay — Slieve Foye mountain, ancient ring forts and the mythological Táin Trail

things.name: Ravensdale Forest Park
things.description: Beautiful forest park in the Cooley Mountains with walking and cycling trails through native woodland and mountain streams

things.name: St Patrick's Cathedral
things.description: Impressive Victorian Gothic cathedral housing remarkable stained glass and the tomb of Oliver Plunkett's headless body

things.name: Seatown Tower & Town Walls
things.description: The only surviving remnant of Dundalk's medieval town walls — a Dominican priory tower dating from the 14th century

things.name: Newgrange (Day Trip via Drogheda)
things.description: 5,200-year-old UNESCO World Heritage passage tomb — one of the world's greatest prehistoric monuments in the Boyne Valley

things.name: Mourne Mountains (Day Trip, NI)
things.description: Northern Ireland's highest mountains overlooking the Irish Sea — dramatic granite peaks, Tollymore Forest and the Silent Valley reservoir

things.name: Oriel Gallery & Arts Centre
things.description: Dundalk's main arts venue with changing exhibitions, theatre productions and an annual arts festival in the town
